pi_items.source: Task description explicitly states: 'You are a Facility Manager auditing vendor performance.'
Facility Manager role managing building facilities (HVAC, elevators, server rooms, plumbing, generators, fire alarms) is consistent with real estate property management industry.
SLA_Policy.txt explicitly states: 'All "Critical" and "High" priority Work Orders must be RESOLVED within 24 HOURS of being REPORTED.'
All 6 work orders are seeded by setup_task.sh and listed in SLA_Policy.txt TARGET WORK ORDERS section.
Calculated from seeded timestamps in setup_task.sh: 6h <= 24h threshold = COMPLIANT
Calculated from seeded timestamps: 24.5h > 24h threshold = BREACHED. Verifier confirms this logic.
Calculated from seeded timestamps: 23h <= 24h threshold = COMPLIANT
Calculated from seeded timestamps: 32h > 24h threshold = BREACHED

pi_items.verified_value: EXEMPT - Waiting for Parts (Control Board) mentioned in description
pi_items.source: Seeded description explicitly states: 'STATUS: Waiting for Parts (Control Board) - Vendor delay.' Per policy, this exempts from SLA clock.

pi_items.metadata_value: NO TAG (Still open, no resolution time)
pi_items.source: Seeded description: 'Reported: 2026-05-15 12:00 | Issue: Fire alarm panel beeping. Investigation ongoing.' - No Resolved timestamp. Should not be tagged per policy.

privileged_info_summary: This task involves auditing 6 synthetic work orders against a 24-hour SLA threshold. The Facility Manager role in real estate property management must review work orders with timestamps seeded in the OpenMaint system: WO-SLA-001 (6h, COMPLIANT), WO-SLA-002 (24.5h, BREACHED), WO-SLA-003 (23h, COMPLIANT), WO-SLA-004 (32h, BREACHED), WO-SLA-005 (76h but EXEMPT due to 'Waiting for Parts' status), and WO-SLA-006 (unresolved, no tag needed). The task requires prepending SLA compliance tags to work order descriptions based on calculated resolution duration.
